title: ReDoS not documented at evaluate() entry point
description: SCENARIO: A caller who reads only the evaluate() doc comment (lib.rs) does not see any security warning about :regex patterns. The SECURITY notice exists in evaluator.rs str_matches_regex_pat (internal) and in the compile() doc comment. evaluate() is the other public entry point and a user who searches only for evaluate() in the docs will miss the ReDoS warning. MAINTENANCE CONSEQUENCE: A downstream crate evaluates untrusted user-supplied Sieve scripts against messages in a mail service; they miss the warning, ship without a complexity limit, and get DoS'd. The fix is one sentence in the evaluate() doc comment pointing to compile()'s security section.
